verses.text: Le Seigneur connaît bien le psalmiste : il le sonde en profondeur, dans un examen inévitable et parfois douloureux ( **Jb 7.17-19** ).\n\n
L'expression « quand je m'assieds et quand je me lève » renvoie aux activités quotidiennes (cf. **Dt 6.6-9** ).
La connaissance de l'omniscience et l'omnipotence du Seigneur apporte réconfort aux pieux, mais elles emprisonnent le cœur des méchants ( **2.3** ).
La connaissance de l'omniscience et l'omnipotence du Seigneur apporte réconfort aux pieux, mais elles emprisonnent le cœur des méchants ( **2.3** ).
La connaissance de l'omniscience et l'omnipotence du Seigneur apporte réconfort aux pieux, mais elles emprisonnent le cœur des méchants ( **2.3** ).
La merveilleuse science du Seigneur dépasse la capacité du psalmiste à s'en saisir (voir **Jg 13.18** ).
Le psalmiste exprime sa crainte face à l'omniprésence du Seigneur.\n\n
Au séjour des morts : Dieu a accès à toute sa création, même aux lieux les plus difficiles d'accès.

Je prends les ailes de l'aurore : cette expression poétique décrit le voyage vers l'est, jusqu'au lever du soleil, si cela est possible. • L'extrémité de la mer : cette expression emmène le voyageur dans la direction opposée, loin vers l'ouest.

139:10 If I say, surely the darkness shall cover me,.... The darkness of a cloud or of the night, so that my actions shall not be seen; that is, if I entertain such a thought in my mind, that what I do in the dark will escape the sight and knowledge of God, and so be emboldened to commit it; even the night shall be light about me; and make all my works manifest, as light does.

139:11 Yea, the darkness hideth not from thee,.... Any thing that is done by men in it; or "darkeneth not from thee" (i), or causeth such darkness as to hinder the sight of any action committed. The Targum is, "from thy Word;'' see Heb 4:12; but the night shineth as the day; or "enlightens as the day" (k), gives as much light with respect to God as the day does; the darkness and the light are both alike to thee; as is the one, so is the other: the day gives him no more light than the night, and the night no more darkness than the day;

139:12 For thou hast possessed my reins,.... His thoughts and counsels, the reins being the seat of instruction and counsel; hence God is called the trier of the reins, and searcher of the hearts of the children of men; he is the possessor or master of their most secret thoughts, and thoroughly knows them; see Ps 7:9; they are also the seat of the affections, which are naturally sinful and inordinate, and set upon carnal and earthly things; but the Lord possesses and engrosses the affections of his people in the best sense, Ps 73:25;
Les parties délicates et intérieures du corps renvoient à l'être entier. • Même caché dans le ventre de sa mère , le psalmiste était guidé par le Seigneur.

139:14 My substance was not hid from thee when I was made in secret,.... Or "my bone" (n); everyone of his bones, which are the substantial parts of the body, the strength of it; and so some render it "my strength" (o); those, though covered with skin and flesh yet, being done by the Lord himself, were not hid from him; nor the manner of their production and growth, which being done in secret is a secret to men; for they know not how the bones grow in the womb of her that is with child, Ecc 11:5;

139:15 Thine eyes did see my substance, yet being unperfect,.... The word (q) for "substance" signifies a bottom of yarn wound up, or any rude or unformed lump; and designs that conglomerated mass of matter separated in the womb, containing all the essentials of the human frame, but not yet distinguished or reduced into any form or order; yet, even when in this state, the eyes of the Lord see it and all its parts distinctly; and in thy book all my members were written: which in continuance were fashioned, when as yet there was none of them;

139:16 How precious also are thy thoughts unto me, O God!.... The word (r) signifies that which is scarce and rare, and not to be attained and enjoyed; see 1 Sam 3:1; the thoughts and counsels of God are impenetrable and unsearchable; he knows our thoughts, as Aben Ezra observes, but we do not know his, Ps 139:2; as well as it likewise signifies the worth and value of them; God's thoughts are infinitely beyond ours, and infinitely more valuable and more important, and are concerning our welfare and happiness: it is marvellous that God should think of us at all;
Que tes pensées, ô Dieu, me semblent impénétrables : L'examen de Dieu révèle une attention affectueuse. La communion avec le Seigneur offre des richesses indescriptibles.

139:18 Surely thou wilt slay the wicked, O God,.... Since he is God omniscient, and knows where they are, what they have done, are doing, and design to do; and God omnipresent, at hand to lay hold upon them; and God omnipotent, to hold them and inflict due punishment on them; this is a consequence rightly drawn from the above perfections of God. Or "if thou wilt slay the wicked" (z), then, when I awake, I shall be with thee, as Kimchi connects the words;

La crainte du psalmiste le pousse à considérer les méchants et à réfléchir sur lui-même. Le Seigneur, en tant qu'examinateur de tous, juge les méchants pour leurs actes et leurs paroles destructrices. Ainsi, le psalmiste prie pour ne pas être compté parmi eux.\n\n

La crainte du psalmiste le pousse à considérer les méchants et à réfléchir sur lui-même. Le Seigneur, en tant qu'examinateur de tous, juge les méchants pour leurs actes et leurs paroles destructrices. Ainsi, le psalmiste prie pour ne pas être compté parmi eux.\n\n
De la haine : rejeter le mal est un signe de sagesse (voir **1.1** ; **5.5-7** ).

139:22 Search me, O God, and know my heart,.... He had searched him, and knew his heart thoroughly; try me, and know my thoughts; he had tried him, and knew every thought in him, Ps 139:1. This therefore is not said for the sake of God; who, though he is the trier of hearts, and the searcher of the reins, is indeed a discerner of the thoughts and intents of the heart at once, and knows immediately what is in man;
La prière du psalmiste contre les méchants l'amène également à réfléchir sur lui-même : il ne présume pas de sa justice. Cette prière a été reprise par les croyants au fil des siècles.
Le chemin de la sagesse mène à la vie éternelle (voir **1.6** ; **Pr 4.18** ; **6.23** ).
